name: Check Cashing Plus
description_short: Check Cashing Plus operates retail financial services stores in New Jersey, offering check cashing, money transfers, money orders, bill payments, and ancillary services like prepaid cards and passport photos.
description_long:
Check Cashing Plus is a New Jersey-based retail financial services provider with two confirmed locations on Bergenline Avenue (Union City area). The company positions itself as customer-focused and innovative, emphasizing high standards for service quality and community financial access. Founded on principles of integrity and team-based service, the company serves customers who need alternatives to traditional banking.

The company's core service is check cashing for all check types: payroll, government benefits (Social Security, SSI, veteran's benefits, unemployment), insurance, tax refunds, and business checks. Beyond check cashing, they offer Western Union money transfers, money orders, bill payment processing for utilities and credit cards, prepaid MasterCard and Visa debit cards, and ATM access. Additional services include local and international cargo shipping, telephone reloads with prepaid phone sales, notary public and immigration application assistance, passport photos, gift card-to-cash conversion, and internet access workstations for document scanning and email.

Check Cashing Plus distinguishes itself through comprehensive service bundling—combining financial services with practical office and travel document services in single locations. The company emphasizes no-waiting check cashing without bank account requirements and instant bill crediting for many billers. Their rewards program and multi-language support (evident from translation services) suggest attention to their specific community demographic.

The business model remains traditional retail check-cashing with ancillary revenue streams. No pricing, fee structure, or APR information is publicly disclosed on their website. The company has not expanded beyond New Jersey or franchised widely, suggesting either limited growth ambitions or operational focus on their local market. Digital transformation appears minimal—no online check cashing or mobile app is mentioned.
pros: No bank account required to cash checks—instant access to funds without waiting for clearing
Cashes all check types including government benefits, tax refunds, and business checks
Multiple ancillary services in one location: money orders, bill payments, money transfers, passport photos
Instant bill crediting available for many utility and credit card billers
Prepaid debit cards available to avoid credit debt and provide card-like convenience
Western Union money transfer capability for domestic and international funds
Immigration assistance and notary services provide value-add for underbanked immigrant communities
cons: No fee structure, exchange rates, or pricing information disclosed on website—consumers cannot comparison shop before visiting
Limited to two physical locations in New Jersey—not accessible to wider customer base
No digital or online check cashing options—requires in-person visits
Website lacks reviews, testimonials, or third-party verification of service quality or complaints
No transparency on prepaid card fees, reload costs, or bill payment surcharges
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked workers in New Jersey needing immediate check-to-cash conversion without account setup
Immigrant communities needing bundled services: check cashing, money transfers, notary, and immigration assistance
People without checking accounts who need to pay bills quickly and receive instant crediting
Customers seeking cash conversion of prepaid gift cards or need Western Union money transfer services
diagnosis: Check Cashing Plus is best for unbanked or underbanked New Jersey residents and immigrant communities who need immediate check conversion, bill payment services, and bundled ancillary services in one location. The main caveat is complete lack of fee transparency, limited geographic availability (two NJ locations only), and absence of digital/online services—all critical gaps for informed consumer decision-making.
services: Check cashing (payroll, government benefits, insurance, tax refunds, business checks)
Western Union money transfers (domestic and international)
Bill payments (utilities, credit cards, insurance, loans, cable, internet, phone)
Prepaid debit cards (MasterCard and Visa branded)
Telephone reloads and prepaid phone sales
Internet access workstations (scanning, document editing, email, resume services)
